pattern: The destination city never appears as a source; collect all source cities in a set and find the one city not present.
hints: Every city that has an outgoing path appears as the first element of some pair — what about the destination?
Build a set of all cities that have at least one outgoing road; the answer is the only city absent from that set.
One pass to populate the source set, one pass over destinations to find the missing one — O(n) time and space.

edge_inputs.description: paths not given in order; destination is not the last in the list

stress.description: chain of 100 randomly named cities in shuffled order
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    cities = [f'City{i}' for i in range(n + 1)]
    rng.shuffle(cities)
    chain = [[cities[i], cities[i+1]] for i in range(n)]
    rng.shuffle(chain)
    return [chain]
